biography.title: Life and Career of Nguyen Sinh Sac

data.title: Born in Sen Village (Nghe An)
data.description: Nguyen Sinh Sac was born in 1862 in Sen Village, Kim Lien Commune, Nam Dan District, Nghe An Province, into a farming family.

data.title: Orphaned and began his education
data.description: After losing his parents, at age 16 he was adopted by scholar Hoang Xuan Duong, who supported his education. He was known for his intelligence and diligence, and was one of the 'Four Tigers of Nam Dan'.

data.title: Married Hoang Thi Loan
data.description: At age 22, he married Hoang Thi Loan, his life partner during his early years of career and study.

data.title: Passed the Huong Examination (Bachelor degree)
data.description: He passed the regional examination, marking an important milestone in his Confucian academic career.

data.title: Failed the Hoi Examination, continued studying
data.description: He went to Hue to take the metropolitan exam but did not pass. He later worked at the Ministry of Finance and studied at the Imperial Academy, continuing his academic pursuit.

data.title: Earned Pho Bang degree and chose teaching
data.description: He passed the Pho Bang exam. Initially refusing official positions, he lived modestly as a teacher, reading modern texts and interacting with patriotic scholars.

data.title: Served in the Ministry of Rites
data.description: He joined the royal court as a clerk in the Ministry of Rites. He held progressive views, criticized corruption, and emphasized patriotism and compassion for the people.

data.title: District Magistrate of Binh Khe
data.description: He was appointed District Magistrate of Binh Khe (Binh Dinh). He became known for integrity, protecting the poor and opposing local tyrants.

data.title: Demoted after a legal case
data.description: After handling a case involving a local tyrant that resulted in death, he was punished and demoted by the court.

data.title: Moved to Cao Lanh
data.description: He settled in Cao Lanh (Dong Thap), where he taught, practiced medicine, and continued engaging with patriotic scholars.

data.title: Passed away in Cao Lanh
data.description: In late November 1929, Nguyen Sinh Sac passed away in Cao Lanh and was buried near Hoa Long Pagoda. His passing was widely reported, reflecting public respect.

historyOfSite.title: Formation and Development of the Site

data.title: Original grave
data.description: The grave was initially made of earth, later restored by locals using cement, bricks, and tiles.

data.title: Reconstruction
data.description: Soldiers and local people rebuilt the grave with cement, iron railings, and a gravestone.

data.title: Damaged during war
data.description: The grave was damaged by bombings during the war.

data.title: Site construction
data.description: After reunification, construction began on August 22, 1975 and was completed on February 13, 1977.

data.title: Ho Chi Minh Stilt House & Lotus Pond
data.description: A full-scale model of Ho Chi Minh’s stilt house and a lotus pond were built to commemorate his 100th birthday.

data.title: National heritage recognition
data.description: The site was officially recognized as a National Historical and Cultural Relic on April 9, 1992.

data.title: Restoration and expansion
data.description: A major preservation project added exhibition houses and recreated the old Hoa An Village (22,000m²), showcasing traditional Southern lifestyles.

data.title: Temple inauguration
data.description: A temple was inaugurated to honor Nguyen Sinh Sac.

data.title: Memorial ceremony
data.description: People gather annually in a solemn ceremony, expressing gratitude and remembrance.

data.title: Festival upgrade
data.description: The memorial ceremony has been elevated to a provincial-level festival.

data.title: Special National Relic
data.description: The site was recognized as a Special National Relic.

aboutTheSite.title: About the Site
aboutTheSite.description:
Nguyen Sinh Sac Relic Site is a historical and cultural complex in Cao Lanh, Dong Thap, and the resting place of Nguyen Sinh Sac, father of President Ho Chi Minh.

The site combines traditional architecture with a peaceful ecological environment, including the grave area, temple, lotus pond, and recreated Hoa An Village.

It serves not only as a memorial but also as a place to foster national pride and preserve cultural heritage.

aboutNguyenSinhSac.title: About Nguyen Sinh Sac
aboutNguyenSinhSac.description:
Nguyen Sinh Sac (1862–1929) was a patriotic Confucian scholar and an upright mandarin, best known as the father of President Ho Chi Minh.

Despite his academic success, he lived modestly, defended the poor, and spread patriotic ideals through teaching and medicine.

His character and ideology deeply influenced Ho Chi Minh and continue to inspire generations.
